# Meta and Amazon, Meta Shops and ATT, Amazon Logistics and the Anti-Amazon Alliance (Stratechery Update 11-14-2023)

**From:** Ben Thompson <email@stratechery.com>
**Date:** Tue, 14 Nov 2023 11:07:28 +0000
**Source:** inputs/saved_emails/Meta and Amazon, Meta Shops and ATT, Amazon Logistics and the Anti-Amazon Alliance (Stratechery Update 11-14-2023)_Tue,_14_Nov_2023_11-07-28_+0000_18bcd83e0631a6a9.eml
**Processed:** 2025-08-24T19:13:08.712149

Meta and Amazon have made a deal that represents the major shift in the e-commerce landscape that has happened over the last four years, thanks to ATT and Amazon's shift away from being an Aggregator.

View in browser ( https://stratechery.com/2023/meta-and-amazon-meta-shops-and-att-amazon-logistics-and-the-anti-amazon-alliance/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..cik5zmKf87tP0rdqw5XOZ9VlImSHBZ_JAafBYhTanmUkjQzhD-oAbZrCeGe54kdb8rx2tImoXAIFCHfsnsFZkoH2aZtUAEfN-kyoNgdU87_f5CC7Cx_5lSMafNvXmky7QtV-htdQio-ZyfWKLW-piKzbWtX82zdxnJF1CgzoFv5CsXxxbVS7PvcMYvflojRFAdc-MxS6-dVEtXr_TcR-86CGENYfCbDgWOUpLgfDVRV5wXsVz-AENw6CSHg3zqU_53WY20zAqiwTeDRqxHA_uBnnKu4us4LRAS4RUSg3XI-d7jOIRvcajZwtYETszYNf2zEMzqTZivVXyQoH9p_Igw )

( https://stratechery.com )

**********************************************************************************
Meta and Amazon, Meta Shops and ATT, Amazon Logistics and the Anti-Amazon Alliance
**********************************************************************************

( https://stratechery.com/2023/meta-and-amazon-meta-shops-and-att-amazon-logistics-and-the-anti-amazon-alliance/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..cik5zmKf87tP0rdqw5XOZ9VlImSHBZ_JAafBYhTanmUkjQzhD-oAbZrCeGe54kdb8rx2tImoXAIFCHfsnsFZkoH2aZtUAEfN-kyoNgdU87_f5CC7Cx_5lSMafNvXmky7QtV-htdQio-ZyfWKLW-piKzbWtX82zdxnJF1CgzoFv5CsXxxbVS7PvcMYvflojRFAdc-MxS6-dVEtXr_TcR-86CGENYfCbDgWOUpLgfDVRV5wXsVz-AENw6CSHg3zqU_53WY20zAqiwTeDRqxHA_uBnnKu4us4LRAS4RUSg3XI-d7jOIRvcajZwtYETszYNf2zEMzqTZivVXyQoH9p_Igw ) Tuesday, November 14, 2023

Listen to this Update in your podcast player ( https://stratechery.passport.online/member/podcast?url=https%3A%2F%2Frss.stratechery.passport.online%2Ffeed%2Fpodcast%2F6EynvegSpNaDpnt4DJsHzD )

Good morning,

On this morning’s Dithering ( https://dithering.fm/ ) we discussed Humane’s new AI pin. You can add Dithering — two fifteen minute podcasts a week from me and John Gruber — or any of the Stratechery Plus podcasts to your podcast player using the links at the bottom of this email.

On to the Update:

Meta and Amazon
---------------

From Bloomberg ( https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-11-09/amazon-and-facebook-partner-on-new-app-based-shopping-feature ) :

Two of tech’s largest players, Amazon.com Inc. and Facebook parent Meta Platforms Inc., are testing a feature that lets shoppers buy Amazon products directly from ads on Instagram and Facebook. The initiative, which involves asking consumers to link their Amazon accounts to their social-media profiles, could make Meta more attractive to advertisers and let Amazon attract more shoppers from outside its web store.

The unprecedented partnership between the two companies also could help them fend off challenges from TikTok, which has launched a US e-commerce marketplace, as well as such Chinese upstarts and Temu and Shein. US shoppers will see real-time pricing, delivery estimates and product details on select Amazon ads running on Facebook and Instagram, according to an Amazon spokesperson.

This strikes me as a pretty big deal that is downstream of two of the biggest drivers of changes in the e-commerce landscape over the last four years.

Meta Shops and ATT
------------------

Start with the Meta side of things: it was four years ago that Facebook announced Facebook Shops; I drew a connection at the time ( https://stratechery.com/2020/platforms-in-an-aggregator-world/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..hEUFuT5wD0u6513uvtRZmvu_9y0j2gpNBZCMLqlpuIaZ0aRS6vrG6EPSnhMAPHwa6HOXSCA9RSoX-uQXs7j7n61MB9ZGSQQjEuntq7i5L6o6mxodlN7VulpUNkBrHfz_LgJoz59kwOISDN_60w_RCFHAEDz_H1-sQsstCjY1WvUdY1DOnMlw_drlfY5-NaMyIz8wnbydS9su4mBVHsJSdSeI5dudOl4WqwwJYaYT6wtzjZ_GL5Cf-96BGy0wwcnvnBgUDsWNnLPul0TTqQ7Qg64ObSZbyM9Q2g12ST0f37kDZ9mXDNgOW5F70zoeJFVm2x5QdFMpv9tdSuri5RWTCg ) between that announcement and first, the degradation of cookie functionality in the name of privacy, and Apple’s relative degradation of the webview functionality in iOS, which meant that there was no Apple Pay in Facebook’s apps. The end result is that it made perfect sense for Facebook to move conversion into its app, even if it came at the potential long-term expense of Shopify, one of Facebook’s most important partners.

Facebook Shops took on drastically increased importance after the introduction of App Tracking Transparency (ATT) by Apple in 2020. Previously Meta could place a “pixel” on an e-commerce site — something trivially done via the Shopify App store, for example — or an SDK in an app, which perfectly reported conversion information that could be definitively linked to the viewing of a particular ad, without any information leaving Meta; now that link was severed.

One important solution has been to shift Meta’s ad model to probabilistic reporting, and the company is doing well in that regard; the best solution, though, is to shift conversions to happening on Meta’s own properties, instead of on 3rd-party websites. That preserves the old deterministic modeling which is inherently more accurate, which is to say that Meta Shops have become a much bigger priority over the last few years.

This, then, explains why this deal is good for Meta: now Amazon is in Meta Shops too.

Back to the original Article I wrote four years ago, though: at the time some folks at Shopify were adamant that I was framing Facebook Shops in far too negative a light as far as Shopify was concerned: Facebook wasn’t just partnering with Shopify and e-commerce merchants out of convenience, but would absolutely prove to be a good partner that wouldn’t usurp their payments role, which is the part I was most concerned about. That did indeed turn out to be the case: Shopify Payments are integrated into (now) Meta Shops, and Shop Pay is available to all retailers on Meta’s properties. What I failed to fully appreciate at the time is the extent to which Shopify’s focus on merchants as its customers is both genuine and beneficial strategically: merchants want solutions that keep everything in Shopify’s ecosystem because that is how they run their business; to that end, Facebook Shops was only ever going to succeed if it allowed Shopify Payments.

At the same time, it was less essential that Shopify own the conversion itself: sure, it would prefer to have that data, but it was more important that merchants be positioned to succeed. If that meant that Shopify didn’t know who the customer was, that was ok — but Amazon’s calculation is different.

Amazon Logistics and the Anti-Amazon Alliance
---------------------------------------------

I chronicled the second event that happened in 2019 in Day Two to One Day ( https://stratechery.com/2019/day-two-to-one-day/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..Kw-UPtibMK0y0u-H1IPGg13_k0UHoSVZ_ahGFAGw70gnUk_7jpTiTUIJGVNMnlvPp0qqWkapQxIKz3iC-GsnC0aDPDifBjMp71HS62EIvF9xyNxGlDDwuX7IsA_yH3156J7OQ12aTU66m5msp_l9dVjY5I0GirlkvbYLnZm2XRbDeY3Frg9HJbEotyW8q04qsq-QDl6992szcirh7LZsyvltWcg2PFpkITXhWDkZ0sid1JleW5e0gvOkmxxTKpCPgNwN3SSOTQyYu_Zlx4h9WTxjg_GQ_jnkBtexlqbRRY9IhXDJYRYMxwROagAhlyPyo4ePW2ZDXJpvZH2M7t10Yg ) :

What changed is that Amazon decided to travel back in time — to Day One — and invest in what the company does best: massively difficult logistical problems that customers love having solved. Originally that was access to any book, then access to anything period, then access in two days, and now Amazon is committed to one.

That Article explained how Amazon had been slipping into being a Day Two company, driving profits by favoring its products and squeezing suppliers, but that founder Jeff Bezos had reset Amazon’s approach by committing to one-day delivery for Prime members. This started a major build-out that accelerated in the pandemic, to the extent that Amazon overbuilt such that it seriously impaired their earnings. I noted in an Update at the time ( https://stratechery.com/2022/amazon-earnings-amazons-costs-amazon-closes-stores/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..VzYe8mHhTHxVkci0gvcmjwc0-wuYYRFoAgIzGWaw4Eso3TsQuaKD8bN9GuDEgk3-I8DtZ99k6gJrTLTo9RGmTICEtsyrq8nuWdi7GkKIP1jU_Tzh41tKVjK2VYoXaMeWuyWPdEOiprWWeXZOSbDZN9duQ95LjBCCiZC5Wb7Iaap8qx1MJLqkTHzKrP-iu0rBqVMkMXTuReUw9zlcuVgSl5n5e4La9SBvOnjGdXrjbz2ZNr7Pnbi6G1aRn0RPRnxCGdpV2Ma38EkB0cjbYcvKPTwogtbRbrpRuoiTsoviNZAvWFv6dbrx74U1GFU2yNCKCCLg9h461SDlDIPl_LqsLg ) :

What is interesting about this last point is that Amazon seems to have made the same mistake as so many other companies impacted by COVID: management thought that higher growth rates were permanent, but as my highly technical highlighter analysis shows, Amazon’s growth is basically back on trend with the company’s growth rates pre-pandemic:

Amazon trailing-24-month revenue and expenses ( https://stratechery.com/2022/amazon-earnings-amazons-costs-amazon-closes-stores/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..VzYe8mHhTHxVkci0gvcmjwc0-wuYYRFoAgIzGWaw4Eso3TsQuaKD8bN9GuDEgk3-I8DtZ99k6gJrTLTo9RGmTICEtsyrq8nuWdi7GkKIP1jU_Tzh41tKVjK2VYoXaMeWuyWPdEOiprWWeXZOSbDZN9duQ95LjBCCiZC5Wb7Iaap8qx1MJLqkTHzKrP-iu0rBqVMkMXTuReUw9zlcuVgSl5n5e4La9SBvOnjGdXrjbz2ZNr7Pnbi6G1aRn0RPRnxCGdpV2Ma38EkB0cjbYcvKPTwogtbRbrpRuoiTsoviNZAvWFv6dbrx74U1GFU2yNCKCCLg9h461SDlDIPl_LqsLg )

[…] Olsavsky admitted as much:

The last issue relates to our fixed cost leverage. Despite still seeing strong customer demand and expansion of our FBA business, we currently have excess capacity in our fulfillment and transportation network. Capacity decisions are made years in advance, and we made conscious decisions in 2020 and early 2021 to not let space be a constraint on our business. During the pandemic, we were facing not only unprecedented demand, but also extended lead times on new capacity, and we built towards the high end of a very volatile demand outlook.

It’s pretty clear that COVID demand growth did not persist; there wasn’t some large population of folks that started using Amazon during COVID that are now growing their spend with the company, increasing long-term growth rates. If anything it’s starting to feel that for the vast majority of companies COVID didn’t really have any major long-term impact at all: either growth was pulled forward (in the case of subscription businesses) or there was a wave-like surge of demand (for transaction businesses), and the story of 2022 is things returning to the patterns of 2019.

Of course the problem for Amazon is that capacity mistakes cost money — $2 billion of costs this quarter alone, and more for the next few quarters. It’s a reminder that moats built in the real world are so strong precisely because they are so difficult; high reward entails high risk.

That Update was written just under a year after Andy Jassy took over Amazon, but what is notable is that Jassy had already signaled that his response to Amazon’s over-investment would not be to aggressively pare back but rather to double down: Amazon would change from being an e-commerce Aggregator to being an AWS-type of logistics business, with Amazon.com as its anchor customer. From Beyond Aggregation: Amazon as a Service ( https://stratechery.com/2022/beyond-aggregation-amazon-as-a-service/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..Rp6UdJAhoUzzpbBmut25mhOfYXoto9Hk36X1aHyo6eaMnQZuXzGZ_WDsl_gOqcNz0g4fCSseeA03eC9SkANBq-8nhMDQK1hPj1HLw8fyyNFXj_UCJtG3Bkzzoddu5WQ4nUmx9VkZXvzu9m8IrN_zn0R8ieJ92YMorrj8IcJ64u2fsf2ttK_uycsdNFiqPCHyaaTDOnSFJ-O5aND9g1Yu6wStr9kSFW-edgLRzn_U-kj2xHnqTWDyLbL-K-EzVvVbOTIgEVJwZ7xzkemEIvOuEENluPKRMmXY_nrdzwEldraBU_oKtkG31DqkwVwV_dEFyP11qUPl6sHORN2aJrsgsw ) , written after the announcement of Buy With Prime, which made Amazon logistics available to non-Amazon e-commerce sites:

Today Amazon’s logistics is massive and fully integrated from the fulfillment center to the doorstep, even though it only serves Amazon; the obvious next step is opening it up to non-Amazon retailers, and that is exactly what is happening…

Amazon’s control of demand has been — and will continue to be — a tremendous advantage; Amazon not only has power over its suppliers, but it also gets all of the relevant data from consumers, which it can feed into a self-contained ad platform that is untouched by regulation from either governments or Apple.

At the same time, limiting a business to customer touchpoints that you control means limiting your overall addressable market. This may not matter in markets where there are network effects (which means you appeal to everyone) and you are an Aggregator dealing with zero marginal costs (and thus can realistically cover every consumer); in the case of e-commerce, though, Amazon will never be the only option, particularly given The Anti-Amazon Alliance ( https://stratechery.com/2020/the-anti-amazon-alliance/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..pIj5pe2_OlXvUe0XjvKGP5s6ASzXvSNWKyKLtR9DMPgp0UxbC68VYReJ9S4d4S1iv02EjjWtbxXsnawMs5afWBA6W3n-LV9YWZG87TjFXZANUxvs3Ou77xJTLDdbmNNBYWM3d6zZ9FdKTEDjdUMzKlOxkE8iw6ui2L2BEillpFzB9guLo5DSRKfLKRyxH51x2T_0npKzAW3IdAKOsNqs2lkWX0PHF66Wjhq-MwqSqOZosGYROi2EJ4nPsdL_00pfxN1r2EDfdMIZ44uTPxXfTVC7JbhzWA8JAhtzejt3zl5j4krsFSsnprntTUoZjMUCn_aCLuGXaB6ODCqsvXpn2w ) working hard to reach consumers.

Two of the most important members of The Anti-Amazon Alliance ( https://stratechery.com/2020/the-anti-amazon-alliance/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..pIj5pe2_OlXvUe0XjvKGP5s6ASzXvSNWKyKLtR9DMPgp0UxbC68VYReJ9S4d4S1iv02EjjWtbxXsnawMs5afWBA6W3n-LV9YWZG87TjFXZANUxvs3Ou77xJTLDdbmNNBYWM3d6zZ9FdKTEDjdUMzKlOxkE8iw6ui2L2BEillpFzB9guLo5DSRKfLKRyxH51x2T_0npKzAW3IdAKOsNqs2lkWX0PHF66Wjhq-MwqSqOZosGYROi2EJ4nPsdL_00pfxN1r2EDfdMIZ44uTPxXfTVC7JbhzWA8JAhtzejt3zl5j4krsFSsnprntTUoZjMUCn_aCLuGXaB6ODCqsvXpn2w ) were Meta and Shopify, and it is notable that Amazon has now made deals with both; I called the Shopify deal a win-win ( https://stratechery.com/2023/amazon-and-shopify-shopify-and-its-merchants-the-payments-question/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..Qdjp2F40G6AYk0YrTTMp2E9zXUwlYTEPgnHHxk2u0bAuQ_ZICtEZ42ZN9dah55yeYNNa2oAKn7Bq4A4HMQo5gpBN-xMk4orCjrhCqbS7eKGMUeLFFFNPntcdC69Ls3hOjDQy5Ll050anNzIM4CH5s9BHfw6VD7LSSOPLA_jmpvGQqPnX7gfSaNn5Up7QXvCK85FpGdzHIFLyYk9hM2b3Hg3Yp8aPX1MX-jhSmr1jCsL6LV7ozaYfEvM0w_LUh795u-UD7fzsr1I4TRYF8d3ju55knbWKI99uRrC-jbt2lwBYkvoY3UogZseGnnkrRosYwwtnGwgYdXDcXurFR1or3g ) , and said of Amazon specifically:

That would certainly be a big victory for Shopify in this specific battle, but it is of course part of a broader victory for Amazon in the e-commerce war. With this deal Amazon has just dramatically increased the total addressable market for its logistics service, just as AWS long ago dramatically increased the total addressable market for Amazon’s back-end infrastructure, once again giving Amazon even better leverage on its unmatched willingness to invest.

The parameters of that deal — particularly the fact that Amazon seemed much more motivated to make it happen than Shopify — helped key me in on the point I made above about how merchants want their business centered on Shopify. The fact Amazon was so eager to make it work, though, speaks to Amazon’s priorities: volume, first and foremost.

I suspect this also explains this deal: Amazon is ok — eager, in fact — for transactions to happen away from Amazon.com, and that includes transactions on Meta’s platform. The challenge in making this deal, though, are the same challenges that were at play with Shopify: Amazon is providing a guarantee not to merchants but to customers about the quality of delivery, ease of return, etc., and that means knowing something about the customer and the transaction. That, I think, explains why this partnership will require customers to, at some point, link their Amazon and Meta accounts: this will be a barrier at the beginning, but a link only needs to happen once, at which point Meta will be able to perfectly track the transaction even as Amazon gets the data it needs to execute on its logistics promise.

The pay-off is, once gain, a win-win: Meta gets better tracking, and thus better performing ads, while Amazon gets more volume, both as a first order concern in terms of Meta ads, but also as a second order in that letting Meta have the key data will drive even more conversions in the long run. Remember that for all of Amazon ads’ outsized success they are still about skimming money from intent-based search: Meta excels at finding people at the top-of-the-funnel, and introducing them to products they didn’t even know they wanted.

This will, per the original excerpt above, help in terms of competing with Shein and Temu, and perhaps there is a degree of motivation there. I think, though, that Amazon’s commitment to being a logistics provider first, instead of an Aggregator, has fundamentally transformed the contours of competition laid out in The Anti-Amazon Alliance ( https://stratechery.com/2020/the-anti-amazon-alliance/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..pIj5pe2_OlXvUe0XjvKGP5s6ASzXvSNWKyKLtR9DMPgp0UxbC68VYReJ9S4d4S1iv02EjjWtbxXsnawMs5afWBA6W3n-LV9YWZG87TjFXZANUxvs3Ou77xJTLDdbmNNBYWM3d6zZ9FdKTEDjdUMzKlOxkE8iw6ui2L2BEillpFzB9guLo5DSRKfLKRyxH51x2T_0npKzAW3IdAKOsNqs2lkWX0PHF66Wjhq-MwqSqOZosGYROi2EJ4nPsdL_00pfxN1r2EDfdMIZ44uTPxXfTVC7JbhzWA8JAhtzejt3zl5j4krsFSsnprntTUoZjMUCn_aCLuGXaB6ODCqsvXpn2w ) : now Amazon has an essential role to play that is complementary to everyone else in the stack. They do logistics better than anyone else, and making that logistics available to everyone means that everyone is pulling in the same direction.

This Update will be available as a podcast later today. To receive it in your podcast player, visit Stratechery ( https://stratechery.passport.online/member ).

The Stratechery Update is intended for a single recipient, but occasional forwarding is totally fine! If you would like to order multiple subscriptions for your team with a group discount (minimum 5), please contact me directly.

Thanks for being a subscriber, and have a great day!
